review_title: A one Hub solution that could work a bit faster
description: It's a solution that has it all but I would welcome an overhaul of UI and backend optimization for speed. It feels like there's a lot of ancient code in there.
author: Corstiaan S.
pros: It's a one-hub solution. Everything I need is within reach. Task management and planning in the card-wall of tickets are indispensable.
cons: Slow: Opening a page or a ticket can sometimes take seconds of looking at a blank page. When used in meeting this is really annoying. Clutter: It lacks options to clean-up. Since I work with it for 10 years I have 150 projects in my view and no way to hide old projects. New projects appear at the bottom of that list, so scrolling galore! Yes, I could go to the project-owner and ask him to archive, but you know how that goes in large companies and I don't have rights to archive so I'm stuck with over 10 years of old projects. GIT: It could use some more optimizations, specially Merge-request handling / canceling, retaining comments etc.

review_title: Strive to be best toolset for development organization
description: It is ok for smaller teams. It is affordable. Not much of online documentation, but most parts are intuitive.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It is a cloud based solution and can be accessed from anywhere. Support for protocols like git. It has embedded document management workflow.
cons: Lack of support for document search. Lack of support for wide adoption among developers. No easy way of project management, bug tracking etc. At the least the versions that we used in the past. No management reports. It serves the basic purpose of source code management with git. No clear support for command line tools and scripting for ci/cd pipelines.

description: Overall, after not following their 30-day cancellation policy to the letter we were automatically billed for an extra year with zero refund. Not only did nobody there apologize, I was even informed that they are very proud of their commitment to sticking to the policy. I even listened to [sensitive content hidden] tell me about a prior customer who made it all the way to the sensitive content hidden] of the company, but in the end they were also not given one cent back.
author: Christopher D.
pros: It was not hard to use but I would not say it was easy or intuitive either.
cons: By far, the customer service is the worst I have ever been involved with, especially at the management level. At several points during the conversation with a manager named [sensitive content hidden], he even laughed at the situation and specifically at me. The entire staff is in Portugal and from top to bottom I have never been treated so poorly by a company.

description: Other than the ridiculous cost its been extremely reliable for the past 7 years we have used it. We've had a couple short outages but they were very brief and got quick attention from their support.
author: scott S.
pros: Reliable SVN Server, pretty fast, easy to manage
cons: We just use the SVN features, it's ridiculously expensive per person for SVN,.

description: Even with its flaws it's a good option over git and svn, it has a nice webapp to help you keep track of the project and source files.
author: Steffano D.
pros: It seems very secure, and I love the fact that you can see which files others have checked out, that's huge thing when working with a big team.
cons: It's super slow when you need to revert files or when you added 2k files to a submit queue and you decide not to move forward with the submit. That will take a lot of time and it's really annoying. One last thing is that is a pain in the ass for new users especially for artists to setup perforce, perforce should have automated tools to setup your workspace, like a wizard to ask you about the basics. Having to open the command to type manual commands is not intuitive.

description: It has been not as good, the tool is slow and more like an old management tool. Also, the customer service is not very good. We had to report all manager for a whole week the same problem to get a follow up for the problem. For a whole week, all of our teams couldn't see the missing info on tickets, it was a waste of our time and resources.
author: ximena D.
pros: The Kaban and list view are convenient. Being able to add options to tickets are also good for management, and the prioritization set is good too. The way the tickets keep track of the dates and hours of every change in the ticket is really good. And the relationships you can build between tickets is good too.
cons: A don't like many things about this software, the main thing is that Is too hard to plan long-term using it. Also is hard to have access to quantitative reports. To keep all my information accurate and easy to access I use Airtable to organize the information I have on Assembla. Because Assembla is too slow and too hard to get reports from it. Everyone at my office does the same thing of using another tool to interpret and organize the info we have on Assembla. I think a Gantt view would help and a view more likely to excel in which you could add and edit tickets faster. It would be great if you could build features for the admins to build their own statistics and reports. And features to automate some actions on tickets.

description: HORRIBLE CANNOT RECOMMEND I did not even get AN EMAIL just to ACKNOWLEDGE I had reached out to them for help (and presumably someone will eventually get around to responding in this "chat")
author: Michael M.
pros: I... suppose I could paste a public ssh key (and it seems to work)
cons: CANNOT EVEN CHECKOUT ANY REPOSITORIES - not with ssh and NOT with username and password! apparently User Management is a complete PAIN to use with these guys oh and the "chat" says someone will response WITHIN A FEW HOURS
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great Project Management Tool Unfortunately Super-Seeded
description: Tracked and version controlled website releases. Easy oversight of website epics, stories and tasks.
author: Dan O.
pros: Reliability, I've used Assembla for more than 10 years and I do not recall any significant downtime, sure there were some issues but they were typically resolved within an hour. It does work as a method by which to manage your software development, a repository (SVN or GIT) is built into the solution.
cons: It's expensive for what it is, it isn't that versatile (for example, you can't modify the status's to customise your status or workflow) and the pricing recently once the ownership changed hands. Depending on your user count, Bitbucket and JIRA may compare more favourably. The application really hasn't continued to evolve that much since I first signed up 10 years ago.

description: - Good for Agile Development Process - Good for Project Management - Not Suitable for Software Quality Assurance Process
author: Javed I.
pros: - Best for Agile Development Process - Good reporting feature - Fully access to administrator - Notification for the task assigned user
cons: - Expensive as compared to the other project management tools - Customer support is not available 24/7 - Available only for Web App - Mobile Application version is not available - Not suitable for Test Cases management

author: Josip K.
pros: It has deep integration of source control system with their project management tool so you can track commits using the same view/page that you use for tracking ticket comments.
cons: Assembla was acquired by some other company and the first thing they did is to raise the price. In short time, from one of the cheapest products, they become one of the most expensive products. And that change was very fast and ugly; I remember that we needed to add just one additional member to the team and they said to us that we are on legacy plan, and that we need to move to their standard plan, and pay something like 5-10X more for the same service. Instead of paying more, we moved to Jira :-)

description: As an engineer I am satisfied with Assembla reporting capabilities and task tracing. It is helpful to revert/find certain activities for reference.
author: Michal S.
pros: For people who work with computer on a daily basis (not programmers) it is good tool to report work and tasks progress. It has nice integration with Slack communicator. There are also other tricks and integrations that IT people can benefit from.
cons: People who work less on a office-based routine it is harder to report and follow tasks in Assembla (non programmers). While exporting pdfs and attached images are too big files, output file is not clear. It is tricky or impossible to create subspace/subtickets for part-time subcontractors without giving them access to all other tickets in the space/company. You need to spend some time on reporting directly in Assembla (15min minimum) - cos of having things properly reported.

author: randy R.
pros: Task management is our favorite feature. Some users utilize Cardwall and other project tracking tools. But primarily task assignment and tracking.
cons: We always need better ways to document processes. The Wiki provided does not sufficiently serve that purpose. Perhaps it is the limited formatting for complex heirarchical work. Not sure. Other team members use Confluence and claim it to work better for documentation.

review_title: Good Software Development Platform missing a few features
description: Assembla is an easy to use and powerful tool, and a good choice for software dev teams. We used it successfully for a number of years, but as company size increased it felt like the capabilities of the software were not able to keep up. Would have no problems recommending this for a smaller team.
author: Matthew S.
pros: Excellent integration between Git and the Project Management tools
cons: Hard to configure for an environment where many projects exist across multiple clients

review_title: Need more Project Management reporting & Visual appealing interface
author: Moeen A.
pros: Simplicity is good with few handy options.
cons: Should consider Gantt chart & release management features from Jira & other portals. we are gaming company so we wish your interface to be uplifted & soon we will share you something interested based on your own Assembla.

author: Matthew P.
cons: Web Navigation could have some tips and tricks.

author: Alperen P.
pros: It is important for us to track cardwall on each milestone that we aim. In that regard, viewing/drag&drop on the cardwall is the most used part in this product.
cons: I miss the ticket templates in this product as I prefer to have the option to create a ticket template and don't have to write the same stuff over and over again. Our colleague sent a request I assume 2 years ago but still that feature is not there to use.

description: I primarily use Assembla to manage tickets and host documentation via wiki.
author: Ray R.
pros: Assembla is the most versatile project/task management tool I've used. While there is a bit of a learning curve (as with any tool), the layout is fairly intuitive.
cons: It's a minor issue, but formatting text in the Wiki and tickets can be a challenge.

description: It's a simple tool that is overly complex to use.
author: martin S.
pros: Task management in the application is simple and effective.
cons: The UI is bizarre and very old-fashioned. The software feels like it was designed in 2011. There is no tight integration with Github, which is a clear deficiency of Assembla. It is also VERY chatty and seems to send email about everything.

description: In my experience, Assembla is a very easy application to use. I like how you can organize your ticket view to however you want to experience the app. I usually filter it so it only shows tickets assigned to myself so I don't get lost in other people's information. Overall, I would definitely recommend Assembla to someone looking for a productivity tool for the workplace.
author: Ash M.
pros: Organization of ticket view, ability to share information with colleagues, and ease of use.
cons: I wish it had a multiple image upload rather than having to drag the image into the comment box.

review_title: Critical part of our business, highly valued
author: Kim T.
pros: Easy to use, great visibility into where everything is at.
cons: It can be a bit glitchy at times, but issues are resolved quickly.

review_title: Well working platform as an issue tracker
description: Quite ok, gets the job done.
author: Markku V.
pros: Using mainly for reporting tickets. Pretty ok interface for that purpose. Automatic notifications about ticket updated in browser tab's is pretty cool and handy feature.
cons: , however search results provides too much low quality hits - result filtering could be better. Drag and drop support for images and attachments would be nice, pasting clipped screenshots even better.

author: Alena S.
pros: I am QA and I like: - file attachment in the tickets; - possibility to update an existed ticket;
cons: - I don't like the design of UI: non-changeable columns size, font, colors. - It's not possible to create testcases and test runs.

review_title: A great online source control for projects
description: A great software to use for source control on your projects.
author: Andrew H.
pros: Assembla is great at managing large digital projects, not only within an office, but also when working with people remotely. Assembla's source control functionality is what you expect, but I also really like their ticket system when projects can be defined in a very goal oriented way and then tracked through their life.
cons: The price is good for small teams, but once the team starts to get bigger, or you add people who won't be permanent, but you need them added for a short time, it can start to get pricey.

description: I was new to Assembla and learned how to use the program very quickly and easily. I love the use of related child and parent tickets to break down tasks to make them more manageable to keep things organized. I often use the basic coding functions to make text bold or underlined or make lists so users can read the tickets easily. Assembla really helps my team stay organized and assigned projects and tasks.
author: Roxanna E.
pros: VERY easy to use and learn. Child/Parents tickets are great.
cons: I wish there was a desktop notification when there is an update to a ticket I follow. this would help more than email notifications for me.

review_title: Good stuff but slow to the extent that it's annoying to work with
author: Tim C.
pros: Offers about everything I need for my day to day work
cons: Browsing code repositories and fetching file diffs etc is *insanely* slow
